title: Devices and methods for closing openings in tissue structures
abstract: In accordance with the disclosure, devices are provided for closing an opening in tissue. The devices include a proximal portion configured and arranged to occlude the tissue opening, and a distal anchor portion configured and arranged to anchor the device in the tissue opening. If desired, the distal anchor portion can be moved proximally or distally with respect to the proximal portion during implantation. The proximal portion can be configured and arranged to fit into a left atrial appendage of a patient, and further wherein the distal anchor portion is configured and arranged to extend into the left atrial appendage.
claims: Claims 1. A device for closing an opening in tissue, comprising a proximal portion configured and arranged to occlude the tissue opening, and a distal anchor portion configured and arranged to anchor the device in the tissue opening. 2. The device of Claim 1, wherein the distal anchor portion can be moved proximally or distally with respect to the proximal portion during implantation. 3. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion is configured and arranged to fit into a left atrial appendage of a patient, and further wherein the distal anchor portion is configured and arranged to extend into the left atrial appendage. 4. The device of Claim 1, wherein the distal anchor portion is configured and arranged to anchor itself against or into a wall defined by a left atrial appendage. 5. The device of Claim 1, wherein the distal anchor portion is configured and arranged to pierce through a wall of a left atrial appendage and anchor itself against epicardial tissue of the heart. 6. The device of Claim 1, wherein the distal anchor portion is discrete from the proximal portion. 7. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion includes an expandable framework covered by a layer of occlusive material. 8. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion is formed from a self-expanding mesh body formed from overlapping filaments. 9. The device of Claim 8, wherein the anchor portion is formed from an expandable braided disc. 10. The device of Claim 9, further comprising a tether directed through the mesh body and expandable braided disc, wherein applying tension to the tether draws the mesh body and braided disc closer together. 11. The device of Claim 9, wherein the mesh body and braided disc are longitudinally displaceable with respect to one another and are connected by an elastic member configured to pull the mesh body and braided disc toward each other. 12. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion includes a structural framework formed from a plurality of pre- shaped individual wires. 13. The device of Claim 12, wherein the pre- shaped individual wires are coupled to each other by a banding material. 14. The device of Claim 12, wherein the pre- shaped individual wires are coupled to a membrane material that at least partially defines the proximal portion of the device. 15. The device of Claim 14, wherein the pre-shaped individual wires are able to move with respect to each other and are separated and spaced from each other. 16. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion includes a structural framework formed from a laser cut shape memory material. 17. The device of any of Claims 8-16, wherein the proximal portion is formed at least in part from shape memory material. 18. The device of any of Claims 8-17, wherein the proximal portion is covered at least in part by a fabric, polymeric or tissue layer. 19. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion is coaxial with and surrounds the distal anchoring portion. 20. A system including a delivery catheter having a proximal end and a distal end, the distal end of the delivery catheter being coupled to the device of Claim 19, wherein the distal anchoring portion can be pulled with respect to the proximal portion by the delivery system to create tension between the proximal portion of the device and the distal anchoring portion of the device. 21. The system of Claim 20, further comprising a lock for locking the distal anchoring portion to the proximal portion to maintain the relative position of the distal anchoring portion to the proximal portion. 22. The system of Claim 21, wherein the proximal portion and the distal anchoring portion is connected to the proximal portion by way of a hollow tether. 23. The system of Claim 21, where in the proximal portion of the device defines a central lumen therethrough for permitting passage of the distal anchoring portion. 24. The system of Claim 23, wherein the proximal portion includes a recess for receiving the lock when the lock is affixed to the distal anchoring portion. 25. The system of Claim 20, wherein the delivery catheter includes an inner portion that is configured to be advanced distally through the proximal portion of the device until the inner portion of the delivery system contacts an inner wall of the left atrial appendage 26. The system of Claim 25, wherein the inner portion of the delivery catheter includes a hollow passage for advancing the distal anchoring portion of the device therethrough. 27. A method of treating a left atrial appendage, comprising: providing a system according to Claim 20; disposing the distal end of the delivery catheter proximate a left atrial appendage (LAA) of a patient; deploying the proximal portion of the prosthesis in an opening of the LAA to isolate an interior volume of the LAA from the left atrium; anchoring the distal anchoring portion of the device with respect to the LAA; and applying tension between the proximal portion and distal anchoring portion to hold the proximal portion in place in the opening of the LAA. 28. The method of Claim 27, further comprising pulling a portion of the LAA against the proximal portion of the device. 29. The method of Claim 28, further comprising locking the relative position of the proximal portion and distal anchoring portion by applying a lock that locks the proximal portion to the distal anchoring portion. 30. The method of Claim 27, further comprising releasing the tension and adjusting the position of the proximal portion of the device. 31. The method of Claim 27, wherein anchoring the distal anchoring portion of the device includes directing a distal end of the distal anchoring portion through to the epicardium, deploying an anchor radially outwardly, pulling the anchor against the epicardium while pulling the proximal portion of the device toward the anchor. 32. The method of Claim 31, further comprising confirming positioning of the proximal portion in the LAA under direct utilization, and locking the distal anchoring portion to the proximal portion. 33. The method of Claim 32, further comprising directing a cutting catheter to the proximal portion of the device and severing a tether connecting the device to the delivery catheter. 34. The method of Claim 33, wherein the cutting catheter includes a sleeve that is configured to slide along the tether. 35. The device of Claim 1, wherein the proximal portion and distal anchoring portion are connected by a hollow suture material. 36. The device of Claim 34, further comprising a lock secured to the hollow suture material to secure the relative position of the proximal portion and the distal anchoring portion. 37. A delivery catheter including the device of Claim 35 mounted thereto, wherein the delivery catheter includes a push rod or tube slidably disposed inside of the suture material, the push rod or tube being configured to push the distal anchoring member through to the epicardium outside of the LAA.
